title: Opulaster malvaceus
taxonomy: Opulaster malvaceus is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes other flowering plants such as roses and apples. This plant is native to South Africa and is commonly known as the wild hawthorn. It is a shrub or small tree that produces clusters of small, fragrant flowers. Opulaster malvaceus is also known by its synonym, Osteospermum malvaceum. Within the plant kingdom, Opulaster malvaceus is classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm.
uses: Opulaster malvaceus, a shrub native to South Africa, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its leaves and stems are reported to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antioxidant and antifungal activities.
